last_verdict.note: Logic suite green on the i9-13980HX, ~49s. +13 vs prior 1322 from this session's 2 landings (routing Wyoming fix + faithful/reliable inject-mode live demo). 2 pre-existing numpy divide-by-zero RuntimeWarnings in core/endpointing.py (mel filter), not failures.

Pulled gemma3:1b. MEASURED (inject, 4b): first-audio ~2.4-2.6s (endpoint ~1.1s + LLM ~1.2-1.4s + TTS ~50ms); routing splits fast/main correctly; barge 2/2 on 3 scenarios; add-on merge works. (2) search.local/web.search are a 3-entry STUB -- wire SearXNG (web_search config) or gate the ReAct planner off. (4) short-barge (~0.4s 'Stop.') still rejected (barge_in_min_speech_sec=0.4). === PRIOR 2026-06-03 (voice pipeline STT/barge, for context) ===: DONE 2026-06-03 (voice-pipeline live session; 5 landings on main, all green @1322). FULL HANDOFF: docs/session_2026-06-03_voice_pipeline_stt_barge.md (read it first). HEADLINE: STT was garbled because the SenseVoice offline 2nd-pass was SILENTLY OFF (model never downloaded) -> every final was raw streaming-zipformer ('Are you there'->'Ario der'). FIXED by `python -m tools.setup_models --sense-voice` (machine-local model, gitignored -- EACH MACHINE must download it); the loader now WARNs if the backend is set but the model is missing. Also landed: addressing prompt reframe (answers questions again), DTLN onnxruntime thread-bound (aec_num_threads=1 -- fixed CPU 90-100% pegging -> deaf mic + frozen turns), CONFIRM-gate ('yes' answered instead of dead-ending), real_usage EMPTY/inventory. STILL OPEN (continue here, priority order): (1) BARGE-IN talk-over still flaky on the open nonlinear laptop speaker -- DTLN cancels echo for ASR but SUPPRESSES the user's voice during double-talk so the residual-floor gate rejects quiet barges. The real lever: aec_ref_delay_ms is pinned 19ms but the REAL acoustic delay is ~260ms (coherence delay= logs 258-337ms) -- CALIBRATE with tools/echo_probe.py + gate DTLN to run only while self._speaking (sherpa.py ~1161 engage gate has no speaking guard). Or trigger barge on the coherence detector (sees the user) not the DTLN-suppressed residual. (2) ENDPOINTING cut the user off mid-thought -- stopgapped to endpoint_min_silence_sec=1.1/high_confidence_floor=0; proper fix = endpoint_detector='prosody' (Smart Turn model ALREADY on disk at pretrained_models/sherpa/smart_turn/). (3) fast-tier hallucination; (4) a 'stop' KWS fast-path (kws not built). GOTCHAS: the user's C: disk was FULL (0 bytes) -- it CORRUPTED config.local.json to 0 bytes mid-session (I reconstructed it; it's gitignored + machine-local, see handoff for the working tuning values); free disk before live runs. faster-whisper installed in venv for tools/transcribe_run.py ground-truth (cache deleted to free space, re-downloads on use). === PRIOR SESSION (2026-06-03 barge-stop, for context) ===: (A) d53cac7 fix(barge): RC-2 -- stop_speaking() now AUTHORITATIVELY clears _speaking itself instead of leaving it to the playback worker's epilogue; if native tts.generate() wedges the worker never clears _speaking and the assistant goes DEAF for the rest of the session (capture loop keeps continue-ing past ASR; observed speaking=True ~15s after a 2s reply -> 'playback thread did not exit within 1.0s'). Cut path now clears _speaking + re-arms the one-per-run barge latch + resets echo/far-ref/AEC (all idempotent). Regression test = test_stop_speaking_clears_speaking_and_relatches_on_the_cut (wedged-worker case). RC-5 (observability only, no gate change): when VAD hears SUSTAINED speech during playback but the barge gate keeps rejecting it (and no barge fired this run), log once/episode + emit barge_in_rejected metric so 'user talked over but nothing fired' shows in the run bundle. (B) tools/transcribe_run.py -- timestamped ground-truth transcript (faster-whisper int8 CPU) of a run-bundle WAV to line up against the run log's ASR/barge events. (C) chore(logs) 57354e4 -- pruned stale 2026-06-02 bundles, added 2026-06-03 (incl. run-20260603-101952, a silent-mic capture failure kept as a record). NEXT: RC-5 has NO dedicated test (rejection branch is deep in _capture_loop, no light harness) -- add a capture-loop/replay test if it matters. P1 live-hardware story UNCHANGED from 2026-06-02: open-speaker barge-in is a HARDWARE LIMIT (post-AEC residual echo overlaps a normal voice, no clean operating point); reliable path = HEADPHONES. RC-2 removes the DEAFNESS failure mode that was confounding live tests -- re-run `python -m core --engine sherpa`, talk over a long answer, use the new barge_in_rejected metric to tell 'gate rejected me' from 'engine went deaf'. run-20260603-101952 was a SILENT MIC (avg_rms=0.0) = the known intermittent AT2020 USB-mic problem, not a code bug; re-seat/re-select (python -m sounddevice) before the next live session. Prior 2026-06-02 FIFO/AEC context: see docs/session_2026-06-02_playback_fifo_aec_alignment.md. (1) 554b300 -- recovered the PC-interrupted playback rewrite: TTS flows through a callback-driven OutputStream + bounded PlaybackFIFO so the AEC far-end reference is teed from the audio callback at the TRUE playback position (far->near lag now inside DTLN's +/-60ms); barge-in/shutdown flush the FIFO. Adversarial-reviewed (6 minor, 0 blockers); abort/flush contract documented (a true rollback is unsound under concurrent flush/drain), direct _audio_cb tests, pinned notify wake-path. (2) 088e055 -- the interrupt is now 'a bit slower, higher confidence': EchoCoherenceDetector requires coherence_confirm_frames (default 2) CONSECUTIVE over-threshold frames before firing, so a one-off nonlinear-echo spike can't self-interrupt; identity-free (speaker-ID untouched, stays a separate feature). NEXT (P1, needs the mic): AEC on-hardware -- enable aec in config.local.json, calibrate aec_ref_delay_ms + measure post-AEC ERLE with tools/echo_probe.py now that the far-end ref is aligned; live-tune coherence_confirm_frames (2 vs 3) on a real talk-over; then Smart Turn v3 live floor A/B. Latent real-time item in backlog: move coherence note_playback off the audio callback (lock-free SPSC) before raising coherence_ring_ms. (3) AEC CALIBRATION (tools b5ee64a): the FIFO arch fixed the DTLN far-ref sync -- tools.echo_probe ERLE sweep shows cancellation PEAKS at aec_ref_delay_ms=0 (~10-16 dB live) vs 0.8 dB at the old 94ms; built tools/interrupt_suite.py (mic x strategy sweep) + ERLE/override flags in echo_probe. OPEN-SPEAKER BARGE-IN NOW WORKS (self_interruptions=0): config.local.json set to aec dtln ref_delay=0, identity gate OFF (speaker_gate_input=false, interrupt is identity-free), coherence OFF (conflicts with AEC), aec_relaxed_margin_db=6, barge_in_min_speech_sec=0.6. (4) FIRST live test MISSED the real barge -> root cause: _looks_like_user gated the residual vs playback_level (required user LOUDER than the speaker -- wrong post-AEC). FIXED (b33f525): post-AEC barge now gates on loudness_admits(residual vs _ambient_rms) -- echo-only residual ~0.02, real barge ~0.4 (~26dB above floor). Tuned to self_int=0 at input_loudness_margin_db=15 + barge_in_min_speech_sec=0.8. STILL UNVERIFIED (needs a human): that a REAL barge now fires -- run `python -m core --engine sherpa`, talk over a long answer (should cut off after ~0.8s). The residual math says it WILL fire (0.4 >> ambient+15dB ~0.11). If MISSED: lower min_speech->0.5 / loudness margin->12. (5) ITERATION 3 (e04bb8d): live test had GARBLED TTS (wrong speed/pitch) + still missed barge. FIXED playback rate: device rejects 22050 but PortAudio sometimes 'opened' it anyway -> ran at wrong rate; now probes a supported rate via check_output_settings up front and plays at 44100+resample (portable). FIXED floor-creep: _ambient_rms now FREEZES its rise on bursts (>+6dB) so a sustained talk-over can't raise its own bar (self-calibrating). HONEST CONCLUSION: open ALC285 barge-in is a HARDWARE LIMIT -- the post-AEC residual echo spikes (~0.1-0.3) overlap a normal voice (~0.2-0.4) and vary run-to-run, so a level/sustain gate has NO clean operating point (self_int measured non-monotonic across margins). Best-effort config left (margin 18, sustain 0.8). RELIABLE PATH = HEADPHONES (echo-free -> barge trivially works, snappy; min_speech~0.3, margin~6, aec off). Auto-calibration delivered: playback rate (per-device), AEC delay (FIFO -> ~0), noise floor (freeze-on-burst), coherence margin (EWMA) -- but can't beat the nonlinear-speaker echo. (6) SESSION-ANALYSIS FIXES (9dc9a55): voice fix + barge-fire confirmed live (barge_in_latency~0). Fixed 3 problems from run-20260602-235431: (a) AEC ran on EVERY block incl. no-playback -> DTLN distorted CLEAN speech -> ASR garble; now gated on far-end energy (RMS>1e-4). (b) SenseVoice 2nd-pass hallucinated tiny clips ('SO'->'I then.', 'WHAT'->'I.'); asr_final_min_sec 0.0->0.5 so sub-0.5s clips use the streaming final. (c) barge-in still used speaker-ID via speaker_gate_input; made it IDENTITY-FREE (removed identity from _looks_like_user; speaker-ID gates FINALS only now -- fully decoupled). Not code bugs: TTS queue depth 26 on a long answer = working-as-designed (LLM faster than TTS; barge/stop interrupts it; queue has drop-oldest backpressure); RAM/VRAM ~90% = the gemma3:12b+4b model sizes. 1282 passed. See docs/session_2026-06-02_playback_fifo_aec_alignment.md.
